hypothesis: EMA crossovers within Bollinger band compression zones with elevated volume oscillator indicate strong momentum continuation signals, as the combination filters out weak breakouts while leveraging volatility to define entry/exit points

The combination of EMA crossovers with Bollinger compression and volume confirmation filters out weak signals while preserving high-probability momentum breaks. ATR-based risk management ensures dynamic stop-losses that adapt to volatility, reducing unnecessary losses during choppy markets.

The combination of EMA crossovers with Bollinger Band compression and volume confirmation filters out false breakouts while maintaining sensitivity to strong momentum moves. When EMA 50 crosses above EMA 200 in a compressed Bollinger zone with elevated volume, it signals a high-probability continuation move. The ATR-based risk management ensures dynamic stops and profits that scale with volatility, improving risk-adjusted returns.
